title: satureja varia subsp. canariensis
intro: Satureja varia subsp. canariensis, also known as Canarian savory, is a plant species within the mint family that is native to the Canary Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Canarian savory include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Canarian savory can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote overall well-being and relaxation.
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", satureja varia subsp. canariensis benefits male sexual function by delaying ejaculation latency and increasing serum testosterone levels, suggesting its potential as a natural remedy for premature ejaculation and male sexual dysfunction.
